rulings.Case Title: KIM TIMMONS VS. TRADER JOE'S COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 1: Defendant TRADER JOE'S COMPANY, INC.'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION AKA SEE YIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum To B Of A And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf & Their Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 3: Defendant CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION, AKA SEE YIP CHINESE MERCHANTS ASSOCIATION'S Motion Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum to B Of A and Request for Monetary Sanctions against Plaintiff & Their Counsel Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearances required. Counsel should be prepared to discuss the following issues: (1) does a corporation have a right of privacy under the California Constitution; (2) does Defendant's prior production of documents from other bank accounts constitute any form of waiver; and (3) the relevance of the subpoena to Citibank and Bank of America based on the allegations of the complaint and cross-complaint. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDWIN LOW et al VS. SUSANNE LOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Against Susanne Low [Ccp 664.6]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFFs EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO, JENNIFER HOVLAND's Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Against Susanne Low [CCP 664.6]. Motion for judgment and to enforce settlement agreement is granted pursuant to CCP § 664.6. The written Stipulation for Settlement was agreed to between the parties and provided that it was "binding and may be enforced by a motion under Code of Civil Procedure §664.6 or by any other procedure permitted by law in the applicable state or federal court." Defendant Suzanne Low failed to comply with the terms of the Stipulation for Settlement when she failed to sign and deliver a grant deed of her 8 and 1/3% interest in property 809 Columbus to Plaintiff Edwin Low. (Decl. of Edwin Low, Exh. A1 - A4.) Plaintiffs satisfied their obligations under the settlement agreement by making an initial $60,000 payment (Decl. of Woo in Support of Motion, 12-19), providing records recorded by Mr. Woo on the property to Mr. Sheppard (Decl. of Woo in Support of Reply to the Opposition, 28), and by bringing a cashiers' check for $100,000 to the December 10, 2012 and March 18, 2013 meetings between the parties. (Decl. of Woo in Support of Motion, 17-21.) Jennifer Hovland's absence from the settlement negotiations on October 30, 2012, does not render the settlement unenforceable. All litigants to the action signed the written agreement. See Cortez v. Kenneally (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 523. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF OMAHA, A NATIONAL VS. NELLIE C. MUGANDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Resonses By Pltf To Requests To Produce Documents And For Sanctions Against Pltf And Its Counsel James Macleod And The Dunning Law Firm
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 5: Defendant NELLIE MUGANDA'S Motion to Compel Further Responses by Plaintiff to Requests to Produce Documents and for Sanctions against Plaintiff and Its Counsel James Macleod and The Dunning Law Firm Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that the motion to compel is moot in light of the amended responses which were served after the motion was filed. The Court finds that the original responses were insufficient pursuant to CPP §2031.220 and imposes sanctions on Plaintiff in the amount of $300 as the result of Defendant needing to file this motion before the amended responses were served. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: AMELIA VALDEZ VS. ERNESTO AGUIRRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF AMALIA VALDEZ's Application For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied. Ms. Valdez has not shown the probable validity of the claims upon which the attachment is based. The evidence submitted in opposition to the application for attachment refutes the moving papers, and attachment is not an appropriate remedy. See Civil Procedure Code section 484.090(a)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED RIBHI AYESH VS. MICHAEL TIMOTHY BOWEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Michael Bowen'S Motion For The Genuiness Of All Documents And The Truth Of All Matters Specified In Bowen'S Requests For Admissions To Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 8: Defendant MICHAEL BOWEN'S Motion for the Genuiness of All Documents and The Truth of All Matters Specified in Bowen's Requests for Admissions to be Deemed Admitted Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to deem admitted the matters at issue in the RFA's is denied given that responses have now been served and extenuating circumstances existed as the result of Plaintiff's lawyer withdrawing from the case in April. The Court does find that both Plaintiff, and his prior counsel, are responsible for Defendant having to file this motion, and therefore, awards sanctions in the amount of $300. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT E. CARLONI VS. UNUM GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 19, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
